frameworks.name: model-based planning
frameworks.one_line: prospective training decisions optimized over expected deployment outcome

frameworks.name: heuristic decision-making
frameworks.one_line: myopic rules based on current competence, fairness, or versatility

core_question: How do humans decide how to train collaborators when the optimal division of labor depends on future, not current, competence?
key_assumptions: competence is dynamic and develops with training
optimal division of labor changes over training trajectories
decision-makers can prospect about long-term competence outcomes
manipulations: task goals across military-defense scenarios (land vs air attacks)
relative competences of the two teams
key_findings: participants divided labor according to task demands and relative competences
training decisions tracked anticipated deployment roles, not current competence
a Planning model captured choices better than competence, learning-potential, fairness, or versatility heuristics
interpretations: humans allocate training prospectively, optimizing for expected deployment outcome
prospection about collaborator development is central to division-of-labor decisions
